detaileddescription.para: Removes the client on the given channel from the list of UIs.

parameterdescription.para: if non-NULL and there is no UI on the channel, set an error
send an "error_exit" event with 0 status first
UI is expected to connect on stdio.

memberdef.argsstring: (uint64_t channel_id, Integer width, Integer height, Dict options, Error *err) FUNC_API_SINCE(1) FUNC_API_REMOTE_ONLY
memberdef.name: nvim_ui_attach

detaileddescription.para: Activates UI events on the channel.
Entry point of all UI clients. Allows |embed| to continue startup. Implies that the client is ready to show the UI. Adds the client to the list of UIs. |nvim_list_uis()|

simplesect.para: If multiple UI clients are attached, the global screen dimensions degrade to the smallest client. E.g. if client A requests 80x40 but client B requests 200x100, the global screen has size 80x40.

memberdef.argsstring: (uint64_t channel_id, Integer width, Integer height, Boolean enable_rgb, Error *err) FUNC_API_DEPRECATED_SINCE(1)
memberdef.name: ui_attach

memberdef.argsstring: (uint64_t channel_id, Boolean gained, Error *error) FUNC_API_SINCE(11) FUNC_API_REMOTE_ONLY
memberdef.name: nvim_ui_set_focus

briefdescription.para: Tells the nvim server if focus was gained or lost by the GUI.

detaileddescription.para: Deactivates UI events on the channel.
Removes the client from the list of UIs. |nvim_list_uis()|

memberdef.argsstring: (uint64_t channel_id, Integer width, Integer height, Error *err) FUNC_API_SINCE(1) FUNC_API_REMOTE_ONLY
memberdef.name: nvim_ui_try_resize

memberdef.argsstring: (uint64_t channel_id, String name, Object value, Error *error) FUNC_API_SINCE(1) FUNC_API_REMOTE_ONLY
memberdef.name: nvim_ui_set_option

memberdef.argsstring: (RemoteUI *ui, bool init, String name, Object value, Error *err)
memberdef.name: ui_set_option

memberdef.argsstring: (uint64_t channel_id, Integer grid, Integer width, Integer height, Error *err) FUNC_API_SINCE(6) FUNC_API_REMOTE_ONLY
memberdef.name: nvim_ui_try_resize_grid

detaileddescription.para: Tell Nvim to resize a grid. Triggers a grid_resize event with the requested grid size or the maximum size if it exceeds size limits.
On invalid grid handle, fails with error.

parameterdescription.para: The handle of the grid to be changed.

memberdef.argsstring: (uint64_t channel_id, Integer height, Error *err) FUNC_API_SINCE(6) FUNC_API_REMOTE_ONLY
memberdef.name: nvim_ui_pum_set_height

detaileddescription.para: Tells Nvim the number of elements displaying in the popupmenu, to decide [<PageUp>] and [<PageDown>] movement.

parameterdescription.para: Popupmenu height, must be greater than zero.

memberdef.argsstring: (uint64_t channel_id, Float width, Float height, Float row, Float col, Error *err) FUNC_API_SINCE(7) FUNC_API_REMOTE_ONLY
memberdef.name: nvim_ui_pum_set_bounds

detaileddescription.para: Tells Nvim the geometry of the popupmenu, to align floating windows with an external popup menu.

Note that this method is not to be confused with |nvim_ui_pum_set_height()|, which sets the number of visible items in the popup menu, while this function sets the bounding box of the popup menu, including visual elements such as borders and sliders. Floats need not use the same font size, nor be anchored to exact grid corners, so one can set floating-point numbers to the popup menu geometry.

memberdef.argsstring: (uint64_t channel_id, String event, Object value, Error *err) FUNC_API_SINCE(12) FUNC_API_REMOTE_ONLY
memberdef.name: nvim_ui_term_event

detaileddescription.para: Tells Nvim when a terminal event has occurred
The following terminal events are supported:

listitem.para: "termresponse": The terminal sent an OSC or DCS response sequence to Nvim. The payload is the received response. Sets |v:termresponse| and fires |TermResponse|.

briefdescription.para: Pushes data into RemoteUI, to be consumed later by remote_ui_flush().

memberdef.argsstring: (RemoteUI *ui, Integer grid, Integer width, Integer height)
memberdef.name: remote_ui_grid_resize

memberdef.argsstring: (RemoteUI *ui, Integer grid, Integer top, Integer bot, Integer left, Integer right, Integer rows, Integer cols)
memberdef.name: remote_ui_grid_scroll

memberdef.argsstring: (RemoteUI *ui, Integer rgb_fg, Integer rgb_bg, Integer rgb_sp, Integer cterm_fg, Integer cterm_bg)
memberdef.name: remote_ui_default_colors_set

memberdef.argsstring: (RemoteUI *ui, Integer id, HlAttrs rgb_attrs, HlAttrs cterm_attrs, Array info)
memberdef.name: remote_ui_hl_attr_define

memberdef.argsstring: (RemoteUI *ui, Integer grid, Integer row, Integer col)
memberdef.name: remote_ui_grid_cursor_goto

briefdescription.para: "true" cursor used only for input focus
emulated cursor used both for drawing and for input focus

memberdef.argsstring: (RemoteUI *ui, Integer grid, Integer row, Integer startcol, Integer endcol, Integer clearcol, Integer clearattr, LineFlags flags, const schar_T *chunk, const sattr_T *attrs)
memberdef.name: remote_ui_raw_line

detaileddescription.para: Flush the internal packing buffer to the client.
This might happen multiple times before the actual ui_flush, if the total redraw size is large!
An intentional flush (vsync) when Nvim is finished redrawing the screen
Clients can know this happened by a final "flush" event at the end of the "redraw" batch.
